fr.recommendations: Enhancing the main building’s accessibility by ensuring the front driveway is clear and wide may facilitate the arrival and setup of emergency vehicles. Improving clearly defined access points and pathways within the landscaped area could assist emergency responders in navigating the terrain efficiently. Consideration for expanding access options at the rear can further allow responders more comprehensive coverage and movement flexibility around the property. Utilizing open terrain strategically for staging areas can support operational effectiveness. Managing this space effectively can serve to reduce potential delays and streamline access and movement for emergency teams.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space around the property through effective vegetation management could be beneficial. Increasing the separation between scattered shrubs and the structure would likely reduce fuel continuity and may help protect against potential fire exposure. Clearing the vegetated perimeter and managing vegetation adjacent to the structure could minimize the risk of fire spread close to improvements. Further management of the vegetated access route and ensuring it remains clear would support firefighter access and suppression operations. Maintaining or expanding the open fuel break may decrease the chances of fire spread across the property. Addressing these areas in a managed, thoughtful approach could enhance wildfire resilience and property preparedness over the long term.
